title: Gated live learner-facing Q&A assistant (full guardrail port)
context: A live learner-facing Q&A assistant is backlog-only and explicitly scoped out at launch. It would require porting the full editorial/product-neutrality/not-advice guardrail layer to a runtime LLM endpoint — a much higher safety bar than the authoring-only AI usage. The assistant must refuse individualized advice, investment/crypto/tax/legal questions, product recommendations, and affiliate/lead-gen requests in every interaction; every refusal must emit a correct non-commercial redirect. It must never collect or transmit a learner's personal financial data. This task requires a governance decision before starting and is gated on all core curriculum and QA tasks being complete.
objective: Design, implement, and safety-review a gated live learner-facing Q&A assistant with the full guardrail layer ported to a runtime LLM endpoint — including refusal handling, non-commercial redirects, privacy controls, and a red-team evaluation — pending a governance decision to proceed.
acceptanceCriteria: Governance decision to proceed is recorded before any implementation begins; the decision includes a safety and scope assessment

The assistant refuses and correctly redirects (to free nonprofit counselor / public consumer-finance agency, never a commercial product) for all refused-use categories: individualized financial advice, investment/crypto/forex/retirement-allocation advice, product/brand recommendations, affiliate/lead-gen requests, tax/legal/accounting/insurance/bankruptcy advice, and get-rich/MLM/'credit-repair' schemes
A failing refusal (one that does not emit a correct non-commercial redirect, or that provides prohibited advice) is a QA failure at the same severity as a product-neutrality linter violation
The assistant never collects, transmits, or stores a learner's personal financial data; no learner financial inputs go to any backend or logging system
Red-team evaluation (adversarial prompts for advice-elicitation, product-steering, and data extraction) passes before release
Runtime guardrail layer (refusal rules, denylist, non-commercial redirect) is maintained separately from the authoring-only AI usage and is independently auditable
output: A merged PR (pending governance approval) adding a gated live learner-facing Q&A assistant with the full runtime guardrail layer, privacy controls, and a passing red-team evaluation report.
